channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Critique] Zoom warns of critical account takeover vulnerability
item.description: Zoom is warning of a critical vulnerability in its desktop client and software development kit for Windows that could be exploited by an unauthenticated party to hijack accounts. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Trump administration unveils AI-supported clearinghouse for cyber vulnerabilities
item.description: The Gold Eagle program will allowe industry, critical infrastructure operators and the government to use artificial intelligence to rapidly detect, prioritize and patch cybersecurity vulnerabilities, officials said.

item.title: [Faible] TuxBot v3 Evolution Shows Signs of LLM-Assisted IoT Botnet Development
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a previously unreported Internet-of-Things (IoT) botnet framework dubbed TuxBot v3 Evolution that shows signs of being developed with assistance from a large language…

item.title: [Critique] Google Gemini CLI abused as a hacking agent, malware botnet operator
item.description: A Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" used Google's open-source Gemini CLI AI tool as a hacking agent and to operate a small-scale botnet. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Kosc Telecom : près de 4 000 diagnostics et accès fibre dans une fuite de données
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Kosc Telecom a été revendiquée le 14 juillet 2026. Le dépôt annoncé... L’article Kosc Telecom : près de 4 000 diagnostics et accès fibre dans une fuite de données est apparu en premier…

item.title: [Élevé] DoinSport : les données personnelles, adresses et profils de mineurs en fuite après une cyberattaque
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à DoinSport a été revendiquée le 14 juillet 2026. L’auteur affirme avoir récupéré... L’article DoinSport : les données personnelles, adresses et profils de mineurs en fuite après une…

item.title: [Élevé] Tech In Order : fuite de 1 400 comptes B2B, mots de passe hachés et accès administrateurs
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Tech In Order a été revendiquée le 13 juillet 2026. Le dépôt... L’article Tech In Order : fuite de 1 400 comptes B2B, mots de passe hachés et accès administrateurs est apparu en premier…

item.title: [Faible] Trump’s DNI pick grilled about election security, voter fraud
item.description: Senators pressed director of national intelligence nominee Jay Clayton about his stance on the 2020 election and previous statements about voter fraud. Other issues took a back seat.

item.title: [Élevé] 23andMe reaches $18 million settlement with states for massive breach
item.description: A coalition of 42 state attorneys general reached an $18 million settlement with 23andMe for cybersecurity failings that led to a data breach.

item.title: [Faible] ​ ​AsyncAPI npm packages infected with credential-stealing malware
item.description: Five malicious versions of AsyncAPI packages were published to the Node Package Manager (npm) in a supply-chain attack that delivered a remote access trojan with info-stealing capabilities. [...]

item.title: [Faible] OkoBot Malware Framework Injects Seed Phrase Phishing Into Ledger and Trezor Apps
item.description:
A malware framework called OkoBot has been running on Windows machines since April 2025, and one of its modules is built to con hardware wallet owners out of their recovery phrase.
On an infected PC, the request comes…

item.title: [Faible] Dutch police dismantle global crypto investment scam, arrest alleged mastermind
item.description: Authorities said Wednesday that the group operated like a legitimate international business since at least 2021, running about two dozen call centers across several countries and employing more than 700 people who posed…

item.title: [Critique] We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out
item.description: Intruder built an AI-powered "vulnerability vending machine" that combines code slicing with LLMs to automatically discover complex software vulnerabilities. The company explains how the system found and exploited a…

item.title: [Faible] Firefox, Chrome, Adobe, and VMware Updates Fix Multiple Critical Security Flaws
item.description:
Mozilla has released updates to address two critical flaws in Firefox for which it warned that exploit code has been published.
The vulnerabilities are listed below -
CVE-2026-15718, an invalid pointer in the…

item.title: [Faible] LAPD sidelines relationship with license-plate reader company Flock Safety
item.description: The Los Angeles Police Department is the latest U.S. municipal agency to rethink its relationship to ALPR company Flock Safety.

item.title: [Moyen] Microsoft smashes Patch Tuesday record for second successive month
item.description: Vulnerability counts have been surging this year, and Microsoft's mammoth disclosure this week of 622 bugs is larger than the three previous months combined.

item.title: [Faible] SASE Has An AI Blind Spot. Inspecting Packets Is No Longer Enough.
item.description:
For years, routing traffic through cloud proxies was good enough. Then work moved to the browser, AI entered the workflow, and the inspection model stopped keeping up.
Enterprise workflows now live across SaaS…

item.title: [Critique] Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
item.description:
Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse) has released a new proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit called LegacyHive.
It has been described as a Windows User Profile Service arbitrary hive load elevation of…

item.title: [Faible] New Webinar: Closing the Approval Gap in AI-Era Ad Tech
item.description:
A single approved marketing tag can quietly load fourth-party code your security team has never seen, granting full access to your forms, customer data, and checkout pages.
This on-demand webinar reveals how this…

item.title: [Critique] Cursor Flaw Lets Malicious Cloned Repositories Trigger Windows Code Execution
item.description: Open a repository in Cursor on Windows and, if a file named git.exe is sitting in the project root, Cursor runs it. No click, no approval dialog, no warning that anything in the folder is about to…

item.title: [Moyen] CISA warns admins to patch actively exploited SharePoint flaws
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) warned Tuesday that attackers are actively exploiting three vulnerabilities to hack Internet-exposed on-premises SharePoint Server instances. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] Compromised AsyncAPI npm Packages Deliver Multi-Stage Botnet Malware
item.description:
Four compromised npm packages in the @asyncapi namespace have been observed distributing a multi-stage botnet loader, according to findings from OX Security, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity.
The affected packages are…

item.title: [Critique] First VPN santionné par la justice américaine
item.description: Un VPN connu et deux internautes accusés de soutenir des groupes de rançongiciel.

item.title: [Faible] Doxbin : un faux administrateur condamné au pays de Galles
item.description: Un jeune internaute condamné pour swatting avait usurpé l’identité du véritable administrateur d'un site pirate.

item.title: [Faible] Microsoft: Some Dell PCs shut down after recent Windows updates
item.description: Microsoft is blocking this month's Windows 11 security updates on some Dell devices because they are causing shutdowns and performance issues. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Un hébergeur russe au cœur d’un réseau cybercriminel
item.description: Washington accuse la société Media Land d’avoir fourni l’infrastructure ayant soutenu rançongiciels, phishing et fraude.

item.title: [Critique] US charges alleged operators of Russian bulletproof hosting service
item.description: U.S. federal prosecutors have unsealed charges against three Russian nationals, accusing them of providing bulletproof hosting (BPH) services to ransomware gangs that caused over $62 million in damages to victims…

item.title: [Critique] Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
item.description:
SonicWall has warned of active exploitation of two zero-day vulnerabilities impacting Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series appliances, one of which could be exploited to achieve arbitrary command execution.
The…

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2023-4346 — KNX Association KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1 Overly Restrictive Account Lockout Mechanism Vulnerability
item.description: KNX Association KNX Protocol Connection Authorization Option 1 contains an overly restrictive account lockout mechanism vulnerability that could allow an attacker to purge all devices without additional security options enabled and set a BCU key to lock the device.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-46817 — Oracle E-Business Suite Improper Privilege Management Vulnerability
item.description: Oracle E-Business Suite contains an improper privilege management vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated attacker with network access via HTTP to compromise Oracle Payments. Successful attacks of this vulnerability can result in takeover of Oracle Payments.
